macro_events.event_summary: CNBC report warns of a potential jet fuel shortage heading into summer 2026 travel season, which could raise fuel costs and disrupt airline operations.
primary_drivers: Higher jet fuel (crack spread) prices squeeze airline margins since fuel is 20-30% of operating costs
Refiners with jet fuel yield capture wider distillate crack spreads
Potential flight capacity cuts / fare increases if shortage materializes

beneficiaries.rationale: Valero is the largest US independent refiner with significant distillate/jet fuel output; a jet fuel shortage widens crack spreads directly boosting refining margins.
Phillips 66 refining segment benefits from wider jet/distillate crack spreads; Gulf Coast and West Coast refineries supply major airport hubs.
Marathon Petroleum is the largest US refiner by capacity with meaningful jet fuel production; tight supply lifts realized margins.
Delek US operates refineries with distillate exposure; smaller-cap refiners show higher torque to crack spread moves.
PBF Energy is a pure-play refiner with high operating leverage to jet/diesel crack spreads; historically outperforms peers on distillate tightness.

losers.rationale: Southwest Airlines has minimal fuel hedging in recent years and runs a single-fleet domestic-heavy model highly sensitive to jet fuel spikes; fuel ~25% of opex.
American Airlines carries heavy debt load and does not hedge fuel; margin compression from higher jet fuel hits EPS hardest among legacy carriers.
United Airlines unhedged on fuel; summer is peak international long-haul season where fuel burn is highest, amplifying cost exposure.
Delta owns the Trainer refinery which provides some internal hedge, but net airline exposure still dominates; shortage risk pressures unit costs.
JetBlue has weak balance sheet and thin margins; any fuel cost step-up or capacity disruption disproportionately hits profitability.
Alaska Air is leisure/West Coast exposed for summer travel; fuel shortage could force capacity cuts during peak revenue period.

headlines: What a looming jet fuel shortage could mean for summer travel

macro_events.event_summary: The US benchmark diesel price just posted its biggest weekly drop since late 2022, sharply lowering fuel costs for diesel-intensive industries.
primary_drivers: Lower diesel input cost flows directly to trucking/rail/logistics operating margins via fuel expense line
Fuel surcharge lag — carriers collect surcharges set on prior higher prices while paying lower pump costs, expanding spread short-term
Negative for refiners with heavy distillate exposure as crack spreads compress

beneficiaries.rationale: Knight-Swift is a pure-play truckload carrier; fuel is ~15-20% of operating costs and fuel surcharge programs lag spot diesel, so a sharp drop widens margin in the near term before surcharges reset.
Werner Enterprises — same TL carrier mechanics as KNX, diesel is a top-3 cost line and surcharge lag is a direct tailwind to OR (operating ratio).
J.B. Hunt's intermodal and dedicated segments consume large diesel volumes; lower fuel cost improves dedicated contract margin where surcharge pass-through is imperfect.
LTL carrier with meaningful diesel exposure; LTL fuel surcharges are tariff-based and lag spot prices, creating a short-term margin tailwind.
Pure-play LTL; same surcharge-lag mechanism as XPO, and Saia's high operating leverage amplifies fuel cost moves to EPS.
FedEx Ground and Freight burn significant diesel; fuel surcharge resets monthly/weekly on trailing averages so a sharp spot drop benefits near-term margin.
Large diesel-fueled ground fleet; fuel surcharge index lags, and lower diesel also reduces cost for subcontracted linehaul.
C.H. Robinson as a 3PL/broker benefits when carrier fuel costs fall faster than shipper-paid linehaul rates reset, widening net revenue margin per load.

losers.rationale: Phillips 66 has high distillate yield exposure; a sharp drop in diesel prices compresses distillate crack spreads and pressures refining segment earnings.
Valero is the largest US independent refiner with major diesel output; falling diesel benchmark directly hits the 3-2-1 and distillate cracks that drive Valero's earnings beta.
Marathon Petroleum's refining margin is heavily levered to distillate cracks; a benchmark-level diesel drop flows to segment EBITDA via ULSD crack compression.
Delek US — smaller refiner with concentrated distillate exposure and thin margin cushion; more sensitive to crack compression than integrated peers.
Par Pacific — small refiner; diesel crack compression has outsized EPS impact given limited diversification.

headlines: Biggest drop in benchmark diesel price since late 2022
